preview.summary: Two organized, athletic sides open Group E with crucial early points at stake against group favorites Germany. Ivory Coast's quick attacking transitions meet Ecuador's miserly, defensively disciplined unit in a tightly matched contest.
preview.homeForm: Topped CAF Group F unbeaten with the best goal difference in African qualifying; ranked 34th by FIFA.
preview.awayForm: Finished second in CONMEBOL qualifying behind Argentina, conceding just five goals in 18 games; ranked 23rd by FIFA.
preview.homeKey: Sébastien Haller, attacking leader with physical presence and aerial threat.
preview.awayKey: Moisés Caicedo, Chelsea midfielder driving Ecuador's engine room.
storylines: Emerse Faé's AFCON-winning Elephants in their World Cup return
Ecuador's rock-solid defense anchored by Pacho and Hincapié
Loser faces an uphill battle against Germany in Group E
preview.h2h: The nations have rarely met, making this a fresh intercontinental test between African and South American styles.
